message.content: Why does the staging deploy fail with a migrations error but production works?

content.text: Found it: staging runs migrations with a read-only database role. The migration runner needs DDL privileges, so on staging every ALTER TABLE silently fails and the schema drifts. Production uses the owner role, which is why it never reproduces there.

message.content: That explains a lot. Where is the role configured?

content.text: In the deploy chart values. The fix guide the platform team published covers exactly this case: https://example.com/platform/runbooks/staging-migration-role — it walks through granting the migration runner a dedicated DDL role.

message.content: Bookmark that runbook, we'll need it every time we bring up a new environment.

content.text: Saved. Summary: staging migration failures were caused by the read-only DB role; the platform runbook at https://example.com/platform/runbooks/staging-migration-role documents the dedicated DDL role setup for new environments.
